name: none
description: This condition is empty.

name: bring_object_to_area
description: This condition can be used to check if the unit has been brought to the specified area.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be present in the area.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area the unit must be located in.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: bring_object_to_object
description: This condition can be used to check if the first unit has been brought to the second unit.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be brought near the second unit.

attributes.name: unit2_ref
attributes.description: The unit to bring the first unit to.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: own_objects
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player owns at least the specified number of objects matching the given object id or type/class.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of objects the player must own.

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: Only the units with this object id will be counted. When defined, ignores all other filters.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose objects will be counted.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this class.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this type.

attributes.name: include_transformable_units
attributes.description: When enabled, also count units that can transform into this unit.

name: own_fewer_objects
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player owns at most the specified number of objects matching the given object id or type/class, optionally within the specified area.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The maximum number of objects the player must own.

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: Only the units with this object id will be counted. When defined, ignores all other filters

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose objects will be counted.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: When set, only count units in this area, otherwise, counts units on the entire map.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this class

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this type

attributes.name: include_transformable_units
attributes.description: When enabled, also count units that can transform into this unit.

name: objects_in_area
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player owns at least the specified number of objects matching the given object id or type/class or state, within the specified area.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of objects the player must have in the area.

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: Only the units with this object id will be counted. When defined, ignores all other filters

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose objects will be counted.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area to count the units in.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this class

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this type

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: unit_state
attributes.description: Filters the units to count by this state

attributes.name: include_transformable_units
attributes.description: When enabled, also count units that can transform into this unit

name: destroy_object
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified unit has been destroyed.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be destroyed.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: capture_object
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified unit has been captured by the source player.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be captured.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must own the unit.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: accumulate_attribute
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player has at least the specified amount of a given resource.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum amount of the resource the player must have.

attributes.name: resource
attributes.description: The resource to check

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose resources to check.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: research_technology
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player has fully researched the specified technology.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose technology will be checked.

attributes.name: technology_id
attributes.description: The technology to check.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: timer
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified amount of time has passed since the trigger became active.

attributes.name: timer_seconds
attributes.description: The time in seconds that must have passed

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, the condition only succeeds before the specified time has passed

name: object_selected
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified unit is currently selected by the local player.

deprecated.reason: This effect should no longer be used. It will cause desyncs in multiplayer games. Use Object Selected (Multiplayer) instead.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be currently selected.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: ai_signal
description: This condition can be used to check if an AI signal with the specified ID has been sent by an AI script using the `sn-trigger-signal` strategy number.

attributes.name: ai_signal
attributes.description: The AI Signal ID to check

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: player_defeated
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified player has been defeated.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have been defeated.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: object_has_target
description: This condition can be used to check if the first unit's target is the second unit, or a unit matching the given object id or type/class.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit whose target will be checked.

attributes.name: unit2_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be targeted by the first unit. When set, ignores all other filters (including object id).

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: Only the units with this object id will be checked as targets. When defined, ignores type/class filters.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: Filters the units to check as target by this class.

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: Filters the units to check as target by this type.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: object_visible
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified object is currently visible to the local player (within their line of sight or a revealed area).
Note: this condition does not work in multiplayer — use Object Visible (Multiplayer) instead.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be currently visible.

name: object_not_visible
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified object is currently NOT visible to the local player (outside their line of sight and any revealed areas).
Note: this condition does not work in multiplayer.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be currently not visible.

name: researching_tech
description: This condition can be used to check if the source player is currently in the process of researching (but has not yet completed) the specified technology.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose current research activity will be checked.

attributes.name: technology_id
attributes.description: The technology that must currently be in progress of being researched.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: units_garrisoned
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified building or unit has at least the specified number of units garrisoned inside it.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of units that must be garrisoned.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The building or unit that must have the required number of units garrisoned inside it.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: difficulty_level
description: This condition can be used to check if the current game difficulty matches the specified difficulty level.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The difficulty level to check against.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: chance
description: This condition can be used to apply a random probability to whether the trigger fires. Each time the condition is evaluated, it has a percentage chance equal to the quantity of evaluating to true.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The percentage chance (0 – 100) that this condition evaluates to true when checked.

name: technology_state
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified technology is in a particular state (e.g., available, being researched, or fully researched) for the source player.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The technology state to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose technology state will be checked.

attributes.name: technology_id
attributes.description: The technology whose current state will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: variable_value
description: This condition can be used to check if a trigger variable's value satisfies the specified comparison against a given quantity.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The value to compare the variable against.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: variable1_id
attributes.description: The variable whose value will be compared against the quantity.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to use

name: object_hp
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified object's current HP satisfies the specified comparison against a given value.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The HP value to compare the object's current HP against.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit whose current HP will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to use

name: diplomacy_state
description: This condition can be used to check if the diplomacy stance of the source player toward the target player matches the specified stance.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The diplomacy stance to check for.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose diplomacy stance toward the target player will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: target_player
attributes.description: The player toward whom the source player's diplomacy stance will be checked.

name: script_call
description: This condition can be used to evaluate an XS script function. The function must return a boolean, which is used directly as the result of this condition.

attributes.name: xs_function
attributes.description: The XS script function to call

name: object_selected_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified object is currently selected by the specified player. This is the multiplayer-compatible version of Object Selected.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be currently selected.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who must have the unit currently selected.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: object_visible_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified object is currently visible to the specified player. This is the multiplayer-compatible version of Object Visible.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be currently visible.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the unit's visibility will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: object_has_action
description: This condition can be used to check if an object matching the specified criteria is currently performing the specified unit action, optionally targeting a specific unit.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The specific unit to check for the action. When not set, any unit matching the other criteria is checked.

attributes.name: unit2_ref
attributes.description: The unit that must be the target of the action. When not set, any target is accepted.

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: The unit type to check for the action. When not set, any unit type is accepted.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units of this class will be checked by this condition

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be checked by this condition

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: unit_action
attributes.description: The unit action to check

name: or
description: This condition acts as a logical OR between the two conditions immediately surrounding it (one directly above, one directly below). To OR groups of multiple conditions, use AND conditions to chain them together on either side of the OR.

name: ai_signal_multiplayer
description: This condition can be used to check if an AI signal with the specified ID has been sent. This is the multiplayer-compatible version of AI Signal.

attributes.name: ai_signal
attributes.description: The AI Signal ID to check

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: building_is_trading
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified building (e.g., a Market) is currently being actively traded with — i.e., a trade unit is en route to or from it.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The building (e.g., Market) that must be actively trading.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: display_timer_triggered
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified display timer has expired (reached zero). Display timers are created and managed using the Display Timer effect.

attributes.name: timer_id
attributes.description: The display timer to check

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: victory_timer
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified victory timer satisfies a comparison against a given time value. Victory timers are countdown timers associated with certain game modes such as King of the Hill or Wonder races.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The time value (in seconds) to compare the victory timer against.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose victory timer will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to use

attributes.name: victory_timer_type
attributes.description: The type of victory timer to check

name: and
description: This condition acts as a logical AND link between the conditions immediately surrounding it, grouping them together. Chaining multiple AND conditions extends the group, so that the entire group must be true as a unit — typically used to build one side of an OR.

name: decision_triggered
description: This condition can be used to check if a decision dialog (a pop-up choice presented to the player) has been triggered and a specific option was selected by the player.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: decision_id
attributes.description: The decision ID to check

attributes.name: decision_option
attributes.description: The decision option to check

name: object_attacked
description: This condition can be used to check if objects matching the specified criteria have been attacked.

attributes.name: object_id
attributes.description: The unit type that must have been attacked. When not set, any unit type is accepted.

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of matching objects that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player who owns the objects that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: object_group
attributes.description: The units of this class will be checked by this condition

attributes.name: object_type
attributes.description: The units of this type will be checked by this condition

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The specific unit that must have been attacked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: hero_power_cast
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified player's hero unit has used (cast) a hero power.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player whose hero power usage will be checked.

name: compare_variables
description: This condition can be used to compare the values of two trigger variables against each other using the specified comparison operator.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: variable1_id
attributes.description: The first variable whose value will be compared.

attributes.name: comparison
attributes.description: The comparison operator to use

attributes.name: variable2_id
attributes.description: The second variable whose value will be compared against the first.

name: trigger_active
description: This condition can be used to check if the specified trigger is currently enabled (active).

attributes.name: trigger_id
attributes.description: The ID of the trigger whose active state will be checked.

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

name: local_tech_researched
description: This condition can be used to check if a local technology (a civilization-specific upgrade tied to a specific building) has been researched by the source player, optionally scoped to a specific building instance or area.

attributes.name: local_technology_id
attributes.description: The local technology that must have been researched.

attributes.name: source_player
attributes.description: The player for whom the local technology research status will be checked.

attributes.name: unit1_ref
attributes.description: The specific building instance to check for the researched local technology.

attributes.name: area
attributes.description: The area to check. When not set, the entire map is checked

attributes.name: inverted
attributes.description: When enabled, inverts the result of the condition

attributes.name: quantity
attributes.description: The minimum number of buildings that must have researched the local technology.
